calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-17-2017 Continued To Jul-25-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2017 Continued To Jun-06-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL L. BEAN OF THE SMITH SMITH & FEELEY, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-949-263-5920 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-defendant AIX Specialty Insurance Company's demurrer to the first three causes of action in Build Group, Inc.'s second amended cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. There is no possibility of coverage under the terms of the insurance policy. Build Group fails to allege facts to show coverage by estoppel, nor is it reasonably possible that it can do so. AIX is only obligated to defend Build Group "against any 'suit' seeking those damages" and no suit was filed against Build Group. (Foster-Gardner, Inc. v. Nat'l Union Fire Ins. (1998) 18 Cal.4th 857, 886.) AIX has no duty to indemnify because it is only obligated to pay "those sums that the insured becomes legally obligated to pay as damages'" and no order or judgment has been entered that requires Build Group to pay damages. (City of San Diego v. Ace Prop. & Cas. Ins. Co. (2005) 37 Cal.4th 406, 417.) "[T]he insured has the burden of pleading and proving that the insurer's assumption of the defense gives rise to waiver or estoppel." (State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. v. Jioras (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1619, 1628 fn. 8.) Build Group fails to allege sufficient facts to show an estoppel. Build Group does not allege that AIX assumed defense of the claim or that it engaged in any conduct that would reasonably lead Build Group to believe that there was coverage. Exhibit C to the second amended cross-complaint shows that AIX's administrator inquired into the claim. Exhibit D shows that Build Group knew that AIX did not accept the claim because Build Group sought approval to commence the repairs. AIX's silence did not create a reasonable belief that coverage existed. Build Group's reliance on Spray, Gould & Bowers v. Associated International Ins. Co. (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1260 for the proposition that silence can create an estoppel lacks merit. Spray found an estoppel because the insurer had a regulatory duty to speak. Build Group therefore fails to plead a cause of action for breach of contract. The cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing fails because there is no coverage. (Love v. Fire Ins. Exch. (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1136, 1153.) The derivative cause of action for declaratory relief fails for the same reasons. (Ball v. FleetBoston Fin'l Corp. (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 794, 800; CCP 1061 (court may decline declaratory relief where not necessary or proper).) Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
